title: Hydrocotyle peltata
intro: Hydrocotyle peltata, also known as Pennywort or Indian Pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its rounded leaves and creeping stems. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hydrocotyle peltata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Hydrocotyle peltata is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dry extract, often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to boost overall immune function.
taxonomy: Hydrocotyle peltata is classified as a member of the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the genus Hydrocotyle. The genus Hydrocotyle is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as pennyworts or pipeworts. Hydrocotyle peltata is native to Australia and is commonly found in freshwater environments. The species is native to eastern Australia, specifically in New South Wales and Queensland.
uses: Hydrocotyle peltata, also known as Water Spangles or Peltate Pennywort, is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits.
